title: BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE CENTER - 211info
text: BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE CENTER BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a low-barrier day center that offers computers and charging stations, a temporary mailing address, laundry facilities, restrooms, showers, and resources. Access to the day center is in four, 3-hour time blocks. To enter, participants need a ticket, and 25 tickets are distributed for each time block on a first-come, first-served basis. No ticket is required to pick up mail, which is available up to four times per day. Do Good Multnomah runs shelter and bridge housing programs on site to help people continue on their path to permanent housing. Referrals come through the Day Center. 333 SW Park Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Age 18 and older, pets allowed Hours: Day Center: 7 days per week 8am-8pm (intake stops at 7:45pm), closed the 4th Friday of the month from noon-3pm Mail pick up: 9am-10am, noon-1pm, 3pm-4pm, 6pm-7pm Time Block 1: 8am-10:45am Time Block 2: 11am-1:45pm Time Block 3: 2pm-4:45pm Time Block 4: 5pm-7:45pm Email (503) 988-4100 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 988-4100 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: -Day Center: Sign up for an entry ticket at the Behavioral Health Resource Center off-site outreach van located at NW 5th Avenue and Glisan Street (Greyhound Station parking lot) -Shelter and Bridge Housing: Referral is needed, ask the front desk for information -Call or visit website to confirm holiday hours FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Multnomah County Services: Homeless Drop In Centers Laundry Facilities Public Internet Access Sites Public Restrooms Public Showers/Baths Specialized Information and Referral * Homelessness Issues Temporary Mailing Address If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. BEHAVIORAL HEALTH RESOURCE CENTER
